calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2023 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:20:40 a.m. pausing at 10:36:25 a.m. resuming at 10:39:29 a.m. and concluding at 10:45:37 a.m.

Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau Inc. Represented by Eric Jun, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Raymond Garcia not appearing.

LUTHER JAO - Custodian of Records, is sworn to testify on behalf of the Plaintiff via Zoom Video Conference.

Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents outline and summary of case.

LUTHER JAO is examined by the Court. Witness attests that the statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The following exhibits are Marked/Admitted:

Plaintiffs Exhibit #1- Workers Compensation Policy (4 pages)
Plaintiffs Exhibit #2- Final Audit Statement

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, the supplemental declaration of the Plaintiff, and the affirmation of Plaintiff's witness, the Court finds that Defendant is in breach of Contract and Failed to make policy payment as set forth in the policy.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau Inc. and against Raymond Garcia, aka Raymond W Garcia, aka Raymond Wilson Garcia, aka Ray Garcia, aka Ray W Garcia DBA Troon Enterprises; Troon Enterprises Inc. as follows:

1. The Court DENIES the Plaintiff's request for Prejudgment Interest and Attorney's Fees, after the Courts review of the workers compensation policy and policy declaration The Court finds that the policy makes no mention of payments for Attorney's Fees or Interest. The Court finds that Attorney's fees and Interest are Unconscionable and Unfair.
2. Damages: $2,207,350.33
3. Costs: $866.70

TOTAL JUDGMENT $ 2,208,217.03

Proposed judgment signed as modified and filed in open court. (D505)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Feb-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Continued To Jan-25-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for default prove-up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:53:46 a.m., pausing at 10:54:37 a.m., reconvening at 11:20:37 a.m., and concluding at 11:31:51 a.m.

Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc. represented by Eric Jun, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Raymond Garcia Raymond W. Garcia AKA Raymond Wilson Garcia AKA Ray Garcia AKA Ray W. Garcia DBA Troon Enterprises not appearing.

LUTHER JAO is placed under oath on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court examines Plaintiff's counsel re: venue.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents argument in support of proper venue.

Counsel for Plaintiff shall file a supplemental declaration re: venue.
Cour orders matter continued to Jan-25-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for further default prove-up hearing. Plaintiff's supplemental declaration must be filed and courtesy copies delivered to Department 505 by Jan-18-2023.
